query: 自分はトランスかもしれない
intent: A questioning, anxious user who suspects they might be trans and wants guidance on what to do / how to think about it, not a dictionary definition.

query: トランス シス ノンバイナリー 違い
intent: Wants a beginner-friendly explanation of the basic identity vocabulary and how trans/cis/non-binary differ.

query: 性自認とは
intent: Looking up exactly what gender identity / 性自認 means, including the 性自認 vs 性同一性 vs ジェンダーアイデンティティ nuance.

query: 性別違和ってなに
intent: Wants to understand what gender dysphoria feels like and that there's no threshold to be trans — needs the explainer, not a one-line gloss.

query: クエスチョニング
intent: Exact glossary lookup of 'questioning' — the state of not yet knowing one's gender/orientation.

query: Xジェンダー
intent: Japanese-specific non-binary umbrella term; wants to know what it means and where they fit — no dedicated stub, so the gender-spectrum explainer is best.

query: 好きになる性別と自分の性別 関係ある？
intent: Confused about whether sexual orientation and gender identity are the same thing; wants reassurance they're separate axes.

query: インターセックス 第三の性
intent: Common misconception search — thinks intersex means a 'third sex'; the intersex page explicitly corrects this and explains DSD accurately.

query: のんばいなりー
intent: Kana-only spelling of non-binary; wants the definition of the identity term.

query: ホルモン 胸 いつから
intent: Wants to know when breasts start developing on estrogen HRT and the general timeline of changes.

query: フラホル やり方
intent: Asking about DIY/self-administered HRT (フラホル) using common slang; wants the practical+risk explainer, not just a one-line definition.

query: SRS
intent: Looking up sex reassignment surgery; ambiguous between understanding what the surgery is and finding where to get it, but a lay user typing the bare acronym most wants the explainer of what SRS involves.

query: 思春期ブロッカー 副作用 元に戻る
intent: A teen/parent worried whether puberty blockers are reversible and what the effects are; wants the explainer page.

query: 声 高くしたい mtf 練習
intent: Trans-fem user wanting to feminize their voice through practice at home; wants the voice-training explainer, NOT voice surgery.

query: ひげ 永久脱毛 レーザー 電気 どっち
intent: Comparing permanent hair removal methods (laser vs electrolysis) for facial hair; wants the overview that contrasts both, not a single-method glossary stub.

query: 女性ホルモン 病院 探したい
intent: Wants to find a clinic/hospital that provides feminizing hormone therapy.

query: ホルモン始める前に 精子 凍結
intent: Worried about fertility before starting HRT; considering sperm freezing and wants the fertility-preservation explainer.

query: 性転換手術 費用 保険
intent: Asking how much transition surgery costs and whether insurance applies; wants the cost/finance overview, not a surgery technique page.

query: 戸籍 性別 変更 やり方
intent: Wants to know how to legally change the sex marker on their family register (戸籍), the actual procedure and requirements.

query: 特例法 要件 手術なし
intent: Looking for the current requirements under the GID special-cases law (特例法), specifically whether surgery is still required after the 2023 supreme court ruling.

query: 名前 変えたい 改名 トランス
intent: Wants to change their legal first name; needs the family-court name-change (改名) procedure for trans people.

query: カミングアウト したい 親
intent: A (likely young) person wanting guidance on whether and how to come out to family.

query: アウティング とは
intent: Looking up the precise meaning of the term 'outing' (being exposed without consent) — a definition lookup.

query: 子供から打ち明けられた どうすれば
intent: A parent/friend whose loved one just came out to them, wanting to know how to respond well — the receiver's perspective, not their own coming out.

query: しにたい 相談 lgbt
intent: A distressed user in crisis wanting someone to talk to right now — needs hotline numbers and where to reach out.

query: そぎ がいどらいん 学校
intent: Wants official SOGI guidelines for schools/workplaces (kana-spelled SOGI) — a link collection to point to or cite.

query: トイレ どっち 使う 不安
intent: Anxious about which restroom to use day-to-day; wants practical reassurance and tips, not a legal definition.

query: HRT
intent: Bare abbreviation — user wants to know what hormone therapy is and what it does to the body, not a one-line glossary definition

query: ふらほる
intent: Pure-hiragana of フラホル (DIY/self-medicated HRT); a user considering or curious about self-medication wants the real explainer on why it's risky and how to stay safe, not just the term stub

query: GID
intent: Outdated abbreviation (gender identity disorder / 性同一性障害); user is trying to understand the concept of gender dysphoria. No GID glossary stub exists, and the clinic that literally says 'GID 外来' is NOT what they want

query: 声
intent: Single broad word — most users typing just '声' want to learn how to change/train their voice at home, the explainer, not the surgery glossary stub

query: 名前 変更
intent: Broad word — user wants the practical how-to for legally changing their name (家庭裁判所 改名), not the deadname glossary stub

query: ホルモン
intent: Single broad word — a beginner wants an overview of what hormone therapy changes; the effects explainer is the best landing, ahead of any specific drug or clinic

query: MtF
intent: Bare abbreviation — user wants the meaning of MtF (trans woman); this is a definitional lookup so the glossary stub is correct over any explainer

query: せいべついわ
intent: Pure-hiragana of 性別違和 with a tiny dropped vowel; distressed user wants to understand the feeling, so the explainer beats the glossary stub

query: SRS 病院
intent: Abbreviation (sex reassignment surgery) plus broad word 病院 — user wants the list of clinics/hospitals that do surgery, not the term definition
